content:
# Viscous Fingering

**Summary**: A phenomenon where a less viscous fluid bypasses a more viscous one, creating channels of flow.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1995-okasha|OKASHA, 1995]] studied the impact of viscous fingering on displacement efficiency in tarmat reservoirs.

- [[1995-hussain|Hussain, 1995]] analyzed the impact of viscous fingering on oil recovery in carbonate rocks.

## Scientific Context



- [[1995-hussain|Hussain, 1995]] analyzed the impact of viscous fingering on oil recovery in carbonate rocks.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Lower injection rates reduced viscous fingering and water channeling, increasing the period of miscibility and hydrocarbon recoveries. ([[1995-okasha|OKASHA, 1995, p. 149]])


- Viscous fingering causes non-uniform fronts and early breakthrough, leading to lower oil recovery in composite cores compared to individual plugs. ([[1995-hussain|Hussain, 1995, p. 124]])
